text:
Catherine Deneuve, French actress (born 1943), also known as Catherine Dorléac, Catherine Fabienne Dorleac, Catherine Fabienne Dorléac. Attributes include: 
- nominated for: "Academy Award for Best Actress (for work: Indochine ; statement is subject of: 65th Academy Awards ; point in time: 17 Feb 1993)",
 "César Award for Best Actress (point in time: 1981 ; for work: The Last Metro ; statement is subject of: 6th César Awards)",
 "César Award for Best Actress (point in time: 1976 ; for work: Lovers Like Us ; statement is subject of: 1st César Awards)",
 "BAFTA Award for Best Actress in a Leading Role (point in time: 1969 ; for work: Belle de Jour)",
 "César Award for Best Actress (point in time: 1982 ; for work: Hotel America ; statement is subject of: 7th César Awards)",
 "César Award for Best Actress (point in time: 1988 ; for work: Agent trouble ; statement is subject of: 13th César Awards)",
 "César Award for Best Actress (point in time: 1989 ; for work: A Strange Place to Meet ; statement is subject of: 14th César Awards)",
 "César Award for Best Actress (point in time: 1993 ; for work: Indochine ; statement is subject of: 18th César Awards)",
 "César Award for Best Actress (point in time: 1994 ; for work: My Favorite Season ; statement is subject of: 19th César Awards)",
 "César Award for Best Actress (point in time: 1997 ; for work: Thieves ; statement is subject of: 22nd César Awards)",
 "César Award for Best Actress (point in time: 1999 ; for work: Place Vendôme ; statement is subject of: 24th César Awards)",
 "César Award for Best Supporting Actress (point in time: 2006 ; for work: Palais royal !)",
 "César Award for Best Actress (point in time: 2011 ; for work: Potiche ; statement is subject of: 36th César Awards)",
 "César Award for Best Actress (point in time: 2014 ; for work: On My Way ; statement is subject of: 39th César Awards)",
 "César Award for Best Actress (point in time: 2015 ; for work: In the Courtyard ; statement is subject of: 40th César Awards)",
 "César Award for Best Actress (point in time: 2016 ; for work: Standing Tall ; statement is subject of: 41st César Awards)",
 "European Film Award for Best Actress (statement is subject of: 15th European Film Awards ; point in time: 2002 ; for work: 8 Women ; together with: Isabelle Huppert, Emmanuelle Béart, Fanny Ardant, Virginie Ledoyen, Danielle Darrieux, Ludivine Sagnier, Firmine Richard)",
 "European Film Award – People's Choice Award for Best European Film (statement is subject of: 24th European Film Awards ; point in time: 2011 ; for work: Potiche ; together with: Fabrice Luchini, Gérard Depardieu, François Ozon, Jean-Pierre Grédy, Pierre Barillet)"
